---
title: "Corentin Ségalas"
image: "corentin.jpg"
color1: "#5A59A3"
color2: "#C66060"
angle: 130
links:
  - label: Linkedin
    url: "https://linkedin.com/"
  - label: Google Scholar
    url: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=hASyJFUAAAAJ&hl=fr&oi=ao"
  - label: ORCID
    url: "https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6902-003X"
  - label: Github
    url: "https://github.com/crsgls/"  
  - label: Phd thesis
    url: "http://theses.fr/2019BORD0298"
  - label: HAL
    url: "https://cv.archives-ouvertes.fr/corentin-segalas"
  - label: OSF
    url: "https://osf.io/4gz2d/"
output:
  postcards::solana # onofre, solana, jolla
---
 
## Bio

I am a research fellow in medical statistics at the *University Paris-Cité*, member of the *METHODS* team of the *CRESS* research center. My work focus on comparing statistical methods to personalize the duration of a treatment using observational data. 

Previously, I was a research fellow at the *London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine* where I have worked on the issue of missing data when using propensity score based methods to do causal inference from observational data. My PhD was done at the *University of Bordeaux* and aimed at building nonlinear mixed models with random changepoint to describe cognitive decline trajectories of elder people. Since then, I have also taught statistics mainly at MSc level to epidemiology, health data science, engineer and psychology students.

## Fields of interest

Causal inference | Machine learning | Observational data | Mixed models

## Contact

Feel free to reach me by email: corentin.segalas[at]inserm.fr
